data.content:
## Implementation Plan: Update GitHub Actions off Node 20

### Problem
All three workflow files use actions/checkout@v4 (node20). The release workflow additionally uses actions/upload-artifact@v4 (node20) and softprops/action-gh-release@v2 (node20). GitHub will force Node 24 as default on June 2 2026 and remove Node 20 on Sep 16 2026.

oven-sh/setup-bun@v2 already runs on node24 — no change needed.

### Version Mapping

| Action | Current | Target | Runtime Change |
|--------|---------|--------|----------------|
| actions/checkout | v4 | v6 | node20 → node24 |
| actions/upload-artifact | v4 | v6 | node20 → node24 |
| softprops/action-gh-release | v2 | removed → gh CLI | node20 → N/A |
| oven-sh/setup-bun | v2 | v2 (unchanged) | already node24 |

Why checkout@v6 not v5: v6 is the latest stable with node24. v5 was the initial node24 port; v6 adds credential-handling improvement.

Why upload-artifact@v6 not v7: v6 is the clean node24 runtime bump from v4 with identical API. v7 adds new features (direct uploads, ESM) that are unnecessary and increase change surface.

Why remove softprops/action-gh-release: v2.6.1 (latest) still uses node20. PR softprops/action-gh-release#670 to upgrade to node24 is open but unmerged. The gh release upload CLI is a zero-dependency alternative (Go binary, no Node runtime), pre-installed on all GitHub-hosted runners.

### Changes by File

#### 1. .github/workflows/release-runtime-artifact.yml (3 changes)
- Line 28: actions/checkout@v4 → actions/checkout@v6
- Line 39: actions/upload-artifact@v4 → actions/upload-artifact@v6
- Lines 47-53: Replace softprops/action-gh-release@v2 step with gh release upload command using --clobber flag for idempotent re-runs. Uses GITHUB_TOKEN (permissions: contents: write already set).

#### 2. .github/workflows/ci.yml (1 change)
- Line 15: actions/checkout@v4 → actions/checkout@v6

#### 3. .github/workflows/coverage.yml (1 change)
- Line 15: actions/checkout@v4 → actions/checkout@v6

### Verification Strategy

#### A. Static Validation
Run actionlint on all three modified workflow files to catch YAML/expression syntax errors before pushing.

#### B. Build Path (checkout + upload-artifact)
Push changes to main and trigger workflow_dispatch on the release workflow. This exercises: checkout@v6, setup-bun@v2, build script, upload-artifact@v6. The release upload step is gated by if: github.event_name == 'release' and will be skipped. Verify all 3 matrix jobs (linux-x64, linux-arm64, darwin-arm64) complete. Also verify CI and Coverage Badge workflows pass on the same push.

#### C. Release Asset Upload Path (addresses prior plan review feedback)
1. Create a draft pre-release tagged v0.0.0-ci-test.
2. Publish the draft to trigger the release:published event.
3. Monitor all 3 matrix workflow runs to completion.
4. Verify all 6 expected assets (3 tarballs + 3 sha256 checksums) appear on the release page.
5. Verify no Node 20 deprecation annotations in any workflow run logs.
6. Cleanup: delete the test release and tag.

#### D. Deprecation Annotation Check
In every workflow run from steps B and C, confirm the absence of the Node.js 20 deprecation annotation.

### Complexity and Risk
- Complexity: Low. Two drop-in version bumps plus one action-to-CLI replacement.
- Risk: Low. checkout@v6 and upload-artifact@v6 are API-compatible with v4 (pure runtime bumps). The softprops → gh CLI swap uses a well-established command with existing permissions. --clobber ensures idempotent behavior.
- Dependencies: None. All target versions are released and stable.
- Rollback: Trivial — revert version strings and restore the softprops step.
